channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canada prepares for potential US tariff retaliation amid trade tensions
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has stated Canada will consider all options, including retaliatory measures, in response to the U.S. announcement of new tariffs on Canadian goods. The tariffs, set to take ...

item.title: Niger State Governor Launches Dual Carriageway Reconstruction to Boost Economy
item.description: Niger State Governor, Umaru Bago, has officially started the reconstruction of the 5.43-kilometre Kontagora Township Dual Carriageway, a major infrastructure project aimed at improving transportation ...

item.title: US Lawmakers Seek Trade Probe Into EU Tech Regulations Over Alleged Bias
item.description: A group of US lawmakers has urged President Donald Trump to initiate a formal trade investigation into the European Union's Digital Markets Act and Digital Services Act, arguing that these regulations...

item.title: IIT Roorkee issues advisory on student political participation amid protests
item.description: The Indian Institute of Technology, Roorkee, issued an advisory cautioning students and staff against expressing political affiliations with movements, particularly those currently in public discourse...

item.title: Tundu Lissu Appears in Court for Constitutional Case
item.description: Tundu Lissu, the Tanzanian opposition leader, has appeared in court for a constitutional case, highlighting ongoing political tensions. His presence at the courthouse has drawn attention from supporte...

item.title: Defense Challenges Credibility of NBI Director's Testimony in Sara Duterte Impeachment Trial
item.description: During the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, her defense team questioned the credibility of National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Director Melvin Matibag's testimony regarding alleged...

item.title: AI models reflect gender stereotypes in household task assignments
item.description: An experiment involving chatbots like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini revealed how AI systems perpetuate traditional gender roles in domestic responsibilities. When asked to assign tasks like cooking, cle...

item.title: Laura Loomer Apologizes for Downplaying Ukraine's Struggles After Air Raids
item.description: Laura Loomer, a right-wing influencer and former Trump associate, publicly apologized to Ukraine after experiencing an air raid during her visit to the country. In a post on X, Loomer acknowledged her...

item.title: Tanzanian man accused of murdering partner and fleeing with stepchildren's money
item.description: A Tanzanian man has fled after allegedly tricking his stepchildren into giving him Sh300,000 following the murder of his partner, Leah Mazengo. The body of the 48-year-old woman was found on her farm ...

item.title: Federal Judge Halts Paramount-Warner Bros Merger Over Antitrust Concerns
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily paused the proposed merger between Paramount and Warner Bros. following a legal challenge from a 12-state coalition led by California. The states argue that the deal wo...

item.title: Comelec allows Sara Duterte to run in 2028 unless convicted
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) clarified that Vice President Sara Duterte can still participate in the 2028 presidential elections as long as there is no final conviction in her impeachment pro...

item.title: LHC upholds death sentences for motorway gang rape convicts
item.description: The Lahore High Court (LHC) has confirmed the death sentences awarded to two convicts in the 2020 Lahore-Sialkot Motorway gang-rape case, dismissing their appeals. The court ruled that the prosecution...

item.title: Two individuals face second-degree murder charges in Thunder Bay
item.description: A news article reports that two individuals have been charged with second-degree murder in Thunder Bay, Ontario. The incident, which occurred in the northern part of the province, has sparked signific...

item.title: Kenyan Woman's Mitumba Bale Shock: Discovering Military Uniforms
item.description: A Kenyan woman purchased a second-hand clothing bale for KSh 48,000, only to discover it contained full military camouflage uniforms instead of the casual wear she expected. The incident, captured in ...

item.title: U.S. Ambassador Says Trump Administration Won't Influence Alberta Separation Referendum
item.description: Ambassador Pete Hoekstra, U.S. envoy to Canada, stated during a podcast interview in Edmonton that the Trump administration will not interfere with Alberta's upcoming October 19 referendum on the prov...

item.title: Defense Challenges Ambush Evidence in Cat Matlala's Trial
item.description: In the trial of Vusimuzi 'Cat' Matlala and four co-accused, the defense is challenging the credibility of evidence presented by security guard Bethuel Mzamo Cele regarding the August 2022 ambush of Jo...

item.title: CBN Keeps Interest Rate Steady Amid Global Uncertainties
item.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has decided to keep the Monetary Policy Rate (MPR) at 26.5% following its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting in Abuja. Governor Olayemi Cardoso explained that wh...

item.title: Former Deputy President Warns of Legal Risks for Uhuru if Ruto Wins 2027 Election
item.description: Former deputy president Rigathi Gachagua has warned that former president Uhuru Kenyatta could face imprisonment if President William Ruto secures a second term in the 2027 General Election. The warni...

item.title: Petrol Pump Owners Demand Monthly Pricing and Commission Increase Amid Strike Threat
item.description: Petrol pump owners across Pakistan have announced a nationwide strike following failed negotiations with the government over daily fuel pricing mechanisms. The All Pakistan Petrol Pumps Owners Associa...

item.title: Study reveals alarming decline of rare pookila mouse in Queensland
item.description: A three-year study in southeast Queensland revealed concerning declines in the pookila mouse population, despite deploying 10,666 traps. Researchers found only a single pookila, highlighting potential...
